title: landolphia nitidula
intro: Landolphia nitidula, also known as the African cherry or African yew, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. The medicinal properties of Landolphia nitidula include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of the plant include its use in traditional medicine to treat cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Landolphia nitidula can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
